questions.question: Who is known as the Father of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution and is called its Father.

questions.question: Which fundamental right is called the 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar called Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies) the 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution.

questions.question: The Indian Constitution was adopted on:
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26 November 1949 (Constitution Day/Samvidhan Divas). It came into force on 26 January 1950 (Republic Day). The Constituent Assembly took 2 years, 11 months, 18 days.

questions.question: The Constituent Assembly was chaired by:
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the President (permanent chairman) of the Constituent Assembly. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the chairman of the Drafting Committee and is called the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'.

questions.question: Article 32 is called the 'Heart and Soul of the Constitution' by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar because:
options: It gives the right to move Supreme Court directly for enforcement of Fundamental Rights — right to constitutional remedies
questions.explanation: Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies) allows any citizen to move the Supreme Court directly if their Fundamental Rights are violated. The SC can issue writs (Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Prohibition, Quo Warranto, Certiorari). Ambedkar called it the 'very soul of the Constitution'.

questions.question: The Constitution of India was drafted by the Constituent Assembly in how many days?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly took 2 years, 11 months, and 18 days (December 9, 1946 to November 26, 1949) to draft the Constitution, holding 11 sessions with 165 sitting days.

questions.question: Article 32 is called the 'soul of the Constitution' by:
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar called Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies) the 'heart and soul of the Constitution'. It allows citizens to directly approach the Supreme Court to enforce Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Inter-State River Water Disputes are adjudicated by:
options: Tribunals under Article 262 and Inter-State Water Disputes Act
questions.explanation: Article 262 empowers Parliament to provide for adjudication of disputes relating to use, distribution, or control of inter-state rivers. Parliament enacted the Inter-State River Water Disputes Act 1956.

questions.question: The Constituent Assembly of India was set up under which plan?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Mission Plan (May 1946) provided for the formation of the Constituent Assembly. Elections to the Constituent Assembly were held in July-August 1946.

questions.question: How many sessions did the Constituent Assembly hold before adopting the Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly held 11 sessions over 2 years, 11 months and 18 days (9 December 1946 – 26 November 1949) before adopting the Constitution.

questions.question: The Objectives Resolution was moved in the Constituent Assembly by:
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru moved the Objectives Resolution on 13 December 1946, which outlined the aims and objectives of the Constitution. It was adopted on 22 January 1947.

questions.question: The total number of members who signed the Constitution on 24 January 1950 was:
questions.explanation: 284 members of the Constituent Assembly signed the Constitution on 24 January 1950. The total strength of the Constituent Assembly was 389 (including 4 seats for Chief Commissioners' provinces).

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right was described as the 'heart and soul of the Constitution' by Ambedkar?
questions.explanation: Dr. Ambedkar called Article 32 (Right to Constitutional Remedies) the 'heart and soul of the Constitution' as it gives the right to move the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: The Maneka Gandhi case (1978) expanded the meaning of Article 21 by holding:
options: 'Procedure established by law' must be fair, just, and reasonable
Personal liberty does not include freedom of travel
Article 21 cannot be read with Article 19
questions.explanation: Before Maneka Gandhi, 'procedure established by law' meant any procedure enacted by legislature (Gopalan case, 1950). After Maneka Gandhi, the procedure must be fair, just, and reasonable — linking Art 21 with Art 14 and 19.

questions.question: The directive to provide free legal aid to ensure equal justice is under:
questions.explanation: Article 39A (inserted by 42nd Amendment, 1976) directs the State to ensure equal justice and free legal aid to citizens, especially the poor. Under this, the Legal Services Authorities Act 1987 was enacted.

questions.question: Citizenship in India is dealt with in:
questions.explanation: Part II of the Constitution (Articles 5-11) deals with citizenship at the time of commencement of the Constitution. Parliament enacted the Citizenship Act, 1955 to provide for acquisition and loss of citizenship.

questions.question: The Prevention of Atrocities Act (SC/ST Act) was enacted in:
questions.explanation: The Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 was enacted to prevent crimes against SCs and STs and provide special courts for speedy trial. It was amended in 2015 to strengthen it.

questions.question: The three-tier Panchayati Raj structure recommended by the Balwant Rai Mehta Committee consists of:
options: Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Samiti, Zila Parishad
Gram Panchayat, Block Panchayat, District Board
Village Council, Taluk Board, District Council
Gram Sabha, Gram Panchayat, District Panchayat
questions.explanation: The Balwant Rai Mehta Committee (1957) recommended a 3-tier structure: Gram Panchayat (village level), Panchayat Samiti (block level), and Zila Parishad (district level). This was formally adopted by the 73rd Amendment as the model.

questions.question: The Comptroller and Auditor General's role was described as 'the most important officer under the Constitution' by:
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar described the CAG as the most important officer under the Constitution of India because the CAG ensures financial accountability and that public money is spent as Parliament intended.

questions.question: The Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act was enacted in:
questions.explanation: The FRBM Act, 2003 mandates fiscal discipline by setting targets for reducing fiscal deficit, revenue deficit, and controlling government borrowings. It promotes macro-economic stability and sound public finance management.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the right to move the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Article 32 gives the right to move the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights. Dr. Ambedkar called it the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution.

questions.question: How many members does India's Constituent Assembly have when the Constitution was adopted?
questions.explanation: 284 members of the Constituent Assembly signed the Constitution on 24 January 1950. The full Constituent Assembly had 299 members.

questions.question: The SC/ST Prevention of Atrocities Act was enacted in:
questions.explanation: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act 1989 provides stringent punishment for crimes against SCs and STs. It was amended in 2018 to overcome dilution by Supreme Court.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the resolution of inter-state river water disputes?
questions.explanation: Article 262 provides Parliament can make law for adjudication of disputes relating to waters of inter-state rivers. Under this, Parliament enacted the Inter-State River Water Disputes Act 1956.

questions.question: Article 267 relates to:
questions.explanation: Article 267 establishes the Contingency Fund of India — placed at the disposal of the President for unforeseen expenditures. Parliament has enacted the Contingency Fund of India Act, 1950 for this purpose.

questions.question: Who was the Chairman of the Constituent Assembly that drafted the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the President (Chairman) of the Constituent Assembly. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature borrowed from the Irish Constitution in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP) were borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a:
options: Sovereign, Democratic, Republic with a Unitary bias
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as a Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution are Fundamental Rights guaranteed?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in Part III of the Constitution, covering Articles 12 to 35.

questions.question: The concept of 'Panchayati Raj' was constitutionalized through which Amendment Act?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Directive Principle of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Right to Equality is a Fundamental Right (Article 14), not a Directive Principle.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Election Commissioner and other Election Commissioners.

questions.question: The Supreme Court of India was established under which Article of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 124 provides for the establishment and constitution of the Supreme Court of India.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with 'Local Self-Government' (Panchayats and Municipalities)?
questions.explanation: Part IX deals with Panchayats and Part IXA deals with Municipalities.

questions.question: The idea of 'Judicial Review' in the Indian Constitution is borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: The concept of Judicial Review was borrowed from the Constitution of the United States of America.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj system?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu follows a three-tier Panchayati Raj system with direct elections at all levels.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the nature of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution is federal in form but unitary in spirit, often called quasi-federal.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India as a ...
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as a Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right is guaranteed only to citizens of India?
options: Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression
questions.explanation: Article 19 (Right to Freedom) is available only to citizens, not to foreigners.

questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are ...
questions.explanation: DPSPs are non-justiciable guidelines for the state, not enforceable by courts.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992 is related to ...
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: The judges of the Supreme Court of India are appointed by the ...
questions.explanation: The President appoints Supreme Court judges after consulting the Chief Justice of India.

questions.question: In Tamil Nadu, the intermediate tier of Panchayati Raj is known as ...
questions.explanation: The three tiers are Gram Panchayat (village), Panchayat Union (block), and District Panchayat.

questions.question: The Fundamental Duties were added to the Indian Constitution by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added Part IVA with Fundamental Duties.

questions.question: The concept of a 'Welfare State' in the Indian Constitution is primarily reflected in which part?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles aim to establish social and economic democracy, reflecting the welfare state ideal.

questions.question: The concept of 'Directive Principles of State Policy' in the Indian Constitution was borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy were borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee.

questions.question: The Indian Constitution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on which date?
questions.explanation: The Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: India follows a parliamentary system, not a presidential system.

questions.question: The word 'Socialist' was added to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution are the Fundamental Rights guaranteed?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in Part III, Articles 12 to 35.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: In the Indian Constitution, the power of judicial review is vested in which body?
questions.explanation: The Judiciary, especially the Supreme Court, has the power of judicial review.

questions.question: The idea of 'Residuary Powers' in the Indian Constitution is inspired by which country?
questions.explanation: The residuary powers are vested with the Union, following the Canadian model.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with Local Self-Government (Panchayats)?
questions.explanation: Part IX of the Constitution, added by the 73rd Amendment, deals with Panchayats.

questions.question: The Constitution of India was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on which date?
questions.explanation: The Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949 and came into effect on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in Part III of the Constitution (Articles 12-35).

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are borrowed from the Constitution of which country?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles were inspired by the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment introduced the Panchayati Raj system in India?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) added Part IX for Panchayats.

questions.question: The Election Commission of India is a:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is a constitutional body established under Article 324.

questions.question: Which Tamil Nadu government scheme provides free breakfast to primary school students?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022 for government primary school students.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in India is borrowed from the Constitution of:
questions.explanation: Judicial Review, the power to declare laws unconstitutional, is borrowed from the USA.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a 'Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic'. The words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' were added by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Under the Indian Constitution, the power to conduct elections for the Panchayati Raj institutions is vested in which body?
questions.explanation: The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a High Court in each state?
questions.explanation: Article 214 provides for a High Court for each state.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, added which part to the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX (Panchayats) to the Constitution.

questions.question: In the context of Tamil Nadu, the 'Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994' was enacted to give effect to which constitutional amendment?
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayats Act, 1994, was enacted to implement the 73rd Constitutional Amendment on Panchayati Raj.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution declares India to be a 'Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic'. Which amendment added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular'?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted 'Socialist' and 'Secular' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Directive Principle of State Policy?
questions.explanation: Right to Work is a Directive Principle under Article 41, Part IV, while others are Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, is associated with which institution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX to the Constitution, providing constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions.

questions.question: In the Indian Constitution, the power to conduct elections to the Parliament and State Legislatures is vested in which body?
questions.explanation: Article 324 establishes the Election Commission of India as the independent authority for conducting elections.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in the Indian Constitution is borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: Judicial Review, the power of courts to examine laws, was adopted from the US Constitution.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of Panchayats in every state?
questions.explanation: Article 243, inserted by the 73rd Amendment, mandates the creation of Panchayats at village, intermediate, and district levels.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu government's policy of providing free bus passes to all students up to higher secondary level is an example of implementation of which constitutional principle?
questions.explanation: This policy promotes the Directive Principle under Article 41 (right to education) and Article 45 (early childhood care).

questions.question: Which of the following features of the Indian Constitution is derived from the Government of India Act, 1935?
questions.explanation: The federal scheme, including the distribution of powers between the Centre and states, was largely borrowed from the Government of India Act, 1935.

questions.question: In the context of the Indian Constitution, the term 'We, the People of India' in the Preamble signifies which principle?
questions.explanation: The phrase 'We, the People of India' establishes that the ultimate authority of the Constitution rests with the people, reflecting popular sovereignty.

questions.question: Under the 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, which of the following is a mandatory (compulsory) function of Panchayati Raj Institutions?
options: Preparation of plans for economic development
questions.explanation: Article 243G mandates that Panchayats prepare plans for economic development and social justice, as listed in the Eleventh Schedule.

questions.question: Which of the following features is NOT a part of the basic structure of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Kesavananda Bharati case established the basic structure doctrine; the right to property was removed as a fundamental right by the 44th Amendment.

questions.question: The word 'Socialist' was inserted into the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) added 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the abolition of untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: The DPSP were inspired by the Irish Constitution of 1937.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1992 is related to which subject?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment added Part IX to the Constitution, providing for Panchayats.

questions.question: Who is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: Article 64 states that the Vice President is the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: The High Court of Tamil Nadu is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Madras High Court, established in 1862, is located in Chennai, though it has a bench in Madurai.

questions.question: Which amendment introduced the concept of 'Right to Education' as a Fundamental Right?
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment (2002) inserted Article 21A, providing free and compulsory education for children aged 6–14.

questions.question: The Tamil Nadu Government's policy of reservation in educational institutions and public employment is based on which principle?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu's 69% reservation aims to uplift backward classes, rooted in the principle of social justice upheld by courts.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of the Election Commission of India?
options: Conduct elections to Parliament and State Legislatures
questions.explanation: The President appoints the Chief Election Commissioner; the Election Commission conducts elections, delimits constituencies, and registers parties.

questions.question: Which of the following features of the Indian Constitution is borrowed from the British Constitution?
questions.explanation: The parliamentary system of government is borrowed from Britain.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right prohibits trafficking in human beings and forced labour?
questions.explanation: Article 23 under Right against Exploitation prohibits trafficking and forced labour.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy are:
questions.explanation: Directive Principles are non-justiciable, but fundamental in governance.

questions.question: The Supreme Court of India has original jurisdiction in:
questions.explanation: Article 131 gives the Supreme Court original jurisdiction in disputes between the Union and states or between states.

questions.question: The concept of 'Basic Structure' of the Constitution was propounded in which case?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court in Kesavananda Bharati (1973) held that the basic structure of the Constitution cannot be amended.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the appointment of the Governor of a state?
questions.explanation: Article 155 states that the Governor is appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal.

questions.question: Which terms were added to the Preamble of the Indian Constitution by the 42nd Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' into the Preamble.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution directs the state to secure a uniform civil code?
questions.explanation: Article 44 under the Directive Principles of State Policy asks the state to endeavour to secure a Uniform Civil Code.

questions.question: Under which article can the President suspend the enforcement of Fundamental Rights during a national emergency?
questions.explanation: Article 359 allows suspension of enforcement of Fundamental Rights (except Articles 20 and 21) during a national emergency.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment Act gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) gave constitutional status to rural local self-government (Panchayats).

questions.question: Which landmark case established the basic structure doctrine of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Kesavananda Bharati case (1973) laid down the basic structure doctrine, limiting Parliament's amending power.

questions.question: Which scheme was launched by the Tamil Nadu government to provide free breakfast to primary school children?
questions.explanation: The Chief Minister's Breakfast Scheme was launched in 2022 to provide nutritious breakfast in government primary schools.

questions.question: Which of the following features of the Indian Constitution was borrowed from the Government of India Act 1935?
questions.explanation: The federal scheme, office of Governor, and judiciary were largely borrowed from the Government of India Act 1935.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added the Fundamental Duties?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) inserted Part IV-A with Article 51A listing Fundamental Duties.

questions.question: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act deals with which type of local self-government?
questions.explanation: The 74th Amendment (1992) gave constitutional status to urban local bodies (Municipalities).

questions.question: Which of the following features of the Indian Constitution is borrowed from the Irish Constitution?
questions.explanation: Directive Principles of State Policy are borrowed from the Irish Constitution. Fundamental Rights are from the US Constitution.

questions.question: The concept of 'Equal Protection of Laws' in the Indian Constitution is borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: The concept is borrowed from the 14th Amendment of the US Constitution. Article 14 guarantees equality before law and equal protection of laws.

questions.question: On which date was the Preamble of the Indian Constitution adopted?
questions.explanation: The Preamble was adopted on 26 November 1949 along with the Constitution. It came into effect on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: Under Article 368, the power to amend the Constitution rests with:
questions.explanation: Parliament can initiate amendments, but those affecting federal provisions require ratification by at least half of the state legislatures.

questions.question: The principle of Judicial Review in India is derived from the Constitution of:
questions.explanation: Judicial Review is inspired by the US Constitution. It allows courts to examine the constitutionality of laws.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions for the administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule deals with Scheduled Areas and Tribes. The Sixth Schedule applies to tribal areas in the Northeast.

questions.question: Right to Constitutional Remedies is guaranteed under which Article of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 32 is the 'Right to Constitutional Remedies' allowing approach to Supreme Court. Article 226 provides similar remedy to High Courts.

questions.question: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act (1992) deals with:
questions.explanation: The 74th Amendment added Part IXA for Municipalities, giving constitutional status to urban local bodies.

questions.question: Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee, which drafted the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: In which landmark case did the Supreme Court hold that the Preamble is a part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: In the Kesavananda Bharati case (1973), the Supreme Court ruled that the Preamble is an integral part of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution abolishes untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and its practice in any form is forbidden.

questions.question: Which Directive Principle of State Policy directs the State to secure a uniform civil code?
questions.explanation: Article 44 of the Directive Principles directs the State to endeavour to secure a uniform civil code for all citizens.

questions.question: Which Constitutional Amendment introduced Part IX – Panchayats into the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act, 1992 added Part IX (Articles 243 to 243-O) dealing with Panchayats.

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution provides for the establishment of the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction and control of elections in the Election Commission.

questions.question: What is the age of retirement for a judge of the Supreme Court of India?
questions.explanation: A Supreme Court judge retires at the age of 65 years as per Article 124(2).

questions.question: How many Lok Sabha seats are allocated to the state of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has 39 Lok Sabha constituencies as per the Fourth Schedule.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the term 'Panchayat' defined?
questions.explanation: Article 243 defines 'Panchayat' as an institution of self-government for rural areas.

questions.question: The federal structure of the Indian Constitution is primarily derived from which of the following?
questions.explanation: The federal features, such as the distribution of powers and the office of Governor, were borrowed from the Government of India Act, 1935.

questions.question: Which of the following features of the Indian Constitution is borrowed from the Constitution of Canada?
options: Federal system with a strong centre
questions.explanation: The federal structure with a strong central government is borrowed from Canada, while other features come from different sources.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution are the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs) enumerated?
questions.explanation: DPSPs are covered under Part IV of the Constitution, from Article 36 to Article 51.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right guaranteed by the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Right to Property was originally a Fundamental Right but was removed by the 44th Amendment in 1978 and is now a legal right.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, is associated with which level of local self-government?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment deals with rural local bodies (Panchayats), while the 74th Amendment deals with urban local bodies.

questions.question: The concept of 'Judicial Review' in the Indian Constitution is primarily derived from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: Judicial Review, the power of courts to examine the constitutionality of laws, is borrowed from the USA.

questions.question: Which schedule of the Indian Constitution contains provisions regarding the administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule applies to Scheduled Areas in states other than the northeastern states; the Sixth Schedule applies to tribal areas in the northeast.

questions.question: In the context of elections in India, which authority conducts the election of the President of India?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India conducts elections for the President, Vice President, Parliament, and state legislatures.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of a High Court for a state, including Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Article 214 states that there shall be a High Court for each state; Article 124 deals with the Supreme Court.

questions.question: The idea of Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution was borrowed from which country's constitution?
questions.explanation: The concept of Directive Principles of State Policy was borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution was adopted on which date?
questions.explanation: The Preamble was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26 November 1949, along with the Constitution.

questions.question: Which amendment to the Indian Constitution added the words 'Socialist' and 'Secular' to the Preamble?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act of 1976 inserted 'Socialist', 'Secular', and 'Integrity' into the Preamble.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act is associated with which of the following?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions in India.

questions.question: In the Indian Constitution, the concept of 'Judicial Review' is borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: Judicial Review, the power of courts to review laws, was borrowed from the USA.

questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for the establishment of the Election Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 324 vests the superintendence and control of elections in the Election Commission of India.

questions.question: As per the Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act, the term of office for a Panchayat President is:
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu Panchayati Raj Act provides a 5-year term for Panchayat Presidents, subject to dissolution.

questions.question: The provision of residuary powers with the Centre in the Indian Constitution is borrowed from which country?
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution borrowed the concept of residuary powers with the Centre from the Canadian Constitution.

questions.question: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution is the election of the President conducted?
questions.explanation: Article 54 provides for the election of the President by an electoral college.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble declares India as Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic; 'Federal' is not mentioned.

questions.question: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Indian Constitution are borrowed from which country?

questions.question: Which part of the Indian Constitution contains provisions related to Panchayati Raj?
